title: kewaceae
intro: Kewaceae is a small family of flowering plants, specifically a group of flowering plants that are part of the order of Saxifragales. It is a relatively rare taxonomical rank used to classify plants, typically denoting a more specific grouping within a larger family. Kewaceae contains a limited number of plant species, and this classification is often used to distinguish these plants from other similar species. The name Kewaceae is derived from the Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, a prominent botanical institution that has contributed to the study and classification of plant species.
